2605.25258.title: First, do no harm: Breaking suicidogenic echo chambers in media recommendation
2605.25258.paper_summary: Recommender systems generally optimises user engagement, but this approach is dangerous in mental health contexts. When vulnerable users show signs of suicidal ideation, standard algorithms often trap them in echo chambers of harmful content, worsening their psychological state. In response, we introduce RankAid, a re-ranking method that prioritises clinical safety alongside predictive relevance. It works as an add-on layer to existing models: it penalises risky items and boosts therapeutic content depending on the user's current level of vulnerability. We evaluated this approach using the MovieLens 1M dataset, where items were semantically annotated for clinical risk and therapeutic value using large language models. Our simulations show that our algorithm successfully blocks the recommendation of harmful content during crisis peaks, actively reshaping the feed to support emotional de-escalation. Furthermore, this safety intervention only causes a controlled, acceptable drop in standard accuracy metrics like NDCG. By using asymmetric hyperparameters, RankAid also gives system administrators the flexibility to tune the severity of the intervention based on specific clinical guidelines.
2605.25258.paper_summary_zh: 推薦系統通常優化用戶參與度，但在心理健康的情境下，這種方法是危險的。當脆弱的用戶顯示出自殺意念的跡象時，標準算法往往將他們困在有害內容的回音室中，惡化他們的心理狀態。為此，我們引入了 RankAid，一種重新排序的方法，將臨床安全性與預測相關性置於優先地位。它作為現有模型的附加層運作：根據用戶當前的脆弱程度，對風險項目進行懲罰，並提升治療性內容。 我們使用 MovieLens 1M 數據集評估了這種方法，該數據集中的項目使用大型語言模型進行了臨床風險和治療價值的語義標註。 我們的模擬顯示，我們的算法成功阻止了在危機高峰期間有害內容的推薦，積極重塑了信息流以支持情緒的降級。此外，這一安全干預僅導致標準準確性指標如 NDCG 的可控、可接受的下降。通過使用不對稱超參數，RankAid 還為系統管理員提供了根據特定臨床指導方針調整干預嚴重程度的靈活性。
2605.25258.author: Alberto Díaz-Álvarez et.al.
2605.25258.authors: Alberto Díaz-Álvarez, Raúl Lara-Cabrera, Fernando Ortega-Requena, Víctor Ramos-Osuna
